content:
Welcome back to the weekly market review, covering the Dollar Index, Euro, E-mini S&P (ES), and
NASDAQ. If you have not watched the July 9th, 2023 market review videos on the YouTube channel, please
go back and watch that first. Without having seen that, you will not fully appreciate what is being shared
here. It is assumed you will pause and watch the prerequisite video to fully benefit from the insights
provided.

- FAIR VALUE GAP ANALYSIS
On the weekly chart of the Dollar Index, we discussed this Fair Value Gap. It is completely permissible for
price to trade through it. We mentioned that price would likely draw to the sell side below, and it did,
becoming animated on the downside. Price was just one tick short of reaching the high of the Gap, which is
from the week beginning April 11, 2022, and the high of the candle from the week beginning March 28,
2022. This forms the Fair Value Gap, an Institutional Fair Value Gap or Balanced Price Range. We will
watch to see if it wants to dig into that in the coming week. The previous week's prognostication was very
explosive.

- BREAKER AND CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT
On the daily chart, in the July 9th, 2023 Sunday review and analysis (before the market even opened), it
was mentioned that this is the breaker to watch. We had closed for that week and then opened, trading up
into the breaker. The low to the high midpoint of that is the mean threshold of an order block. For gaps or
inefficiencies, the midpoint is consequent encroachment. We saw price trade up into it, hitting it beautifully,
then delivering to the downside for Monday's trading. On Tuesday, we finally made our way into this Fair
Value Gap and accelerated to the downside, attacking the sell side as anticipated. We expected the return
back into this breaker, and it delivered off that breaker, leading us to anticipate this area here as a sell side
draw.

- LIQUIDITY AND GAPS
There's a common misconception that what I teach is support. No, I am teaching you to view how the
market will draw to that liquidity, to it and through it. Buy side is not resistance; buy side is a draw on
liquidity for markets going higher. We want to tap into buy side liquidity that is above old highs, relative
equal highs, or single highs. It's not a matter of support and resistance; support and resistance is garbage.
I don't believe in support and resistance, but I have methods that teach real dynamic support and
resistance, which are my inefficiencies and gaps. The way I use gaps, they are not filled and done away
with. The market many times will revert back to an old gap's high, low, or consequent encroachment.

section_title: ATTACKING OLD LOWS AND HIGHER TIME FRAME CONTEXT
content:
We do not do trading in supply and demand zones, which are ambiguous. There are three specific price
levels I look at, and if I use the range for targeting to determine if the inefficiency will provide continuation,
I'll put four gradients on that level. It's either a target or an entry, and vice versa. We are not looking at a
level as support to buy from; that's not what I'm doing. Retail traders look at something that resembles
support because I use a rectangle or draw a line on an old low, and they naturally think I'm looking for a
buy there. I don't believe in that theory; I attack old lows. I don't try to find a reason to buy at a low because
the market went up there before. I teach you to anticipate that retail traders with that mindset will want to
buy there, and those already long have sell stops below there. That's perfect if I'm short, because I'm going
to buy below those lows, not at those lows. I want to go through them and reach for targets beyond. It's
nonsensical to say what I teach is classic support and resistance, as I have no faith in it, and neither should
you. The breaker was hit, and we worked off that Weekly Fair Value Gap. In the July 9th video, I mentioned
it doesn't look like there's a gap, which is why you transpose weekly inefficiencies onto your daily chart, so
you don't lose sight of the higher time frame context.

- VOLUME IMBALANCE & ALGORITHMIC DELIVERY
Moving on to Euro Dollar weekly chart. I've been counseling you for months to anticipate this weekly
volume imbalance. This is not in any textbooks, it's not supply and demand, it's not Wyckoff, it's not
anything else out there. You can see how the market has drawn price up into that; that's algorithmic price
action. You will not find a retail book, a retail educator, or anyone who will teach you to anticipate the
market wanting to go back into this area. They don't see it that way. I challenge you to go out and find these
things the way I use them. You're not going to find it prior to me. It's nonsensical to wrestle with the idea
that this has been rebranded, because this is nothing like anything else. This is the marketplace; it's
algorithmically delivered. AI runs the show here, and sometimes manual intervention steps in, which can
make it difficult to profit.

section_title: EURO DOLLAR WEEKLY INVERSION FAIR VALUE GAP & BUY SIDE TARGETS
content:
We looked at this weekly Inversion Fair Value Gap. This is not a liquidity void where you find a sell signal
and sell off. We are looking for this level to act as support, which is what makes this an ICT Inversion Fair
Value Gap. You can see how we opened for the week and traded down. Admittedly, we did not match the
low of this candle with that one, so don't be fooled by the visual representation. For the spread, you would
have been stopped out if you were long. But the idea taught on Sunday, July 9th, 2023, was that it would
trade down to that and then run up to the buy side and into this weekly volume imbalance. This is what your
chart should show for the Euro Dollar weekly chart: a huge, big bullish candle that closed on the high. You
decide if this is made up or cherry-picked.

- WEEKLY INVERSION FVG INTERACTION &
CONTINUATION
Dropping down to a daily chart here. You can see Monday's trading: we opened, traded down towards the
weekly Inversion Fair Value Gap, then rallied. Tuesday had a small, indecisive day, followed by an explosion
on Wednesday, taking the buy side and trading right at the low of the weekly volume imbalance. We then
opened, found some support at the weekly volume imbalance, and rallied. Friday saw a little continuation.
The only thing for this coming week is to see it completely close that in. That's it.

section_title: THE VALUE OF STUDY AND BACKTESTING
content:
When my analysis is shared and the market runs through these levels, delivering as expected, a small sect
of my community immediately goes to Twitter asking 'what's next?' It's as if they're waiting for me to get it
wrong or are not satisfied with the current event for study purposes. There is so much to delve into and
study in these moves for your own edification, rather than just looking for the next opportunity to potentially
criticize. You must go back and backtest all these individual days and trading sessions. This was a very fast
market, doing sudden runs, and the way money is being transferred is fundamentally changing, speeding
things up. There are other global events that might be burdensome for all of us, but we'll leave that out for
now.

Here's the hourly chart. We just fell short of touching the high end of that weekly Inversion Fair Value Gap,
then the market rallied, came back down, found some support in a Fair Value Gap, and rallied again. It
came into another gap and rallied, creating a measuring Gap here. What makes this a measuring Gap? We
are anticipating price getting as high as the shaded area (weekly volume imbalance). We anticipated this
acting as a measure of instigation for a new rally. In simple terms, this is not classic support and resistance,
but I use the idea for contextual understanding. We anticipated the top of this orange-shaded area to act as
support, and then we would look to see it get to the old buy side liquidity. We did not discuss it going there
and coming back down; I said it would go to it, through it, and then trade into this range. The specific levels
within these ranges will be covered in a moment.

On the 15-minute time frame, you can see two down-closed candles, which is a bullish order block
(consecutive down-closed candles). There's a small gap there; extend that forward, and you can see how
the market trades down into it and then rallies. The Inversion Fair Value Gap shows the market finding
support there and rallying up. I have lessons on Inversion Fair Value Gaps, so you'll understand more about
that. I'm laying down a lot of foundation, and you're not going to learn this with one video. Here's that
measuring Gap, moving up into the low of the weekly volume imbalance, and then we find support at the
weekly volume imbalance. That's beautiful. If you had never known about the weekly volume imbalance,
you would never have seen that as a level to anticipate the market wanting to go higher. The market pulls
back down into a small gap, rallies again, and creates a bullish order block here, right into an optimal trade
entry, then continuously moves higher. Some tweets complained that while the market went where
expected (from July 9th, 2023 analysis), they couldn't find an entry. These are the easiest ones in the chart;
there are many smaller time frame entries that could have been utilized. This was a very fast market with
sudden runs. The way money is being transferred is fundamentally changing, speeding things up
expediently.
HOMEWORK:

section_title: FIBONACCI OVERLAY ON WEEKLY VOLUME IMBALANCE
content:
For your homework, as an additional study, you want to take this weekly volume imbalance and lay your
Fibonacci on it from the high to the low. Make sure you get the actual high and low shown from the weekly
chart that makes this weekly volume imbalance. Go back and watch the beginning of the video to see the
candles. You can do a Fibonacci overlay that has the 0, 100, 0.25, 0.50, and 0.75 levels. Study where we
are on the lower time frames (like one minute, five minute charts) every time we traded at or near the 25,
50, and 75 gradients.

- MAY/JUNE OUTLOOK
Moving on to E-mini S&P. This is the seasonal tendency chart for E-mini S&P. I've talked about how I like
seeing the seasonal tendency that comes in the month of May, leading into June. June typically makes a
strong low, and that's just about every single year. I like having a shorting opportunity going into the last
week of April, going into May, and then the majority of May, we see some measure of selling. This year,
while we were anticipating it, the market internals did not present that idea in the technicals. So, while I was
expecting it, we didn't do anything that would get us short and take a loss. We were seeing the market
show us that it's not likely the seasonal tendency was coming to fruition this year. This little movement here,
that's what I was looking at. That is a really easy breadwinner for the springtime, and it was not there
technically.

I want to take your attention to how the third week of May and the third week of June. This right here tends
to be one of the best times to be a buyer of index futures for E-mini S&P. So if you're looking for a strong
mid-year point to buy in a bull market that you feel is going to continue, because we didn't see a seasonal
tendency come to fruition here, I counseled you to do this in my mentorship. If seasonal tendencies don't
form, if they don't come to fruition, that's telling you something extremely fundamental to the next drive
higher or lower. If it's not moving in tandem with what you would expect in terms of the seasonal tendency,
that means it's doing something opposite. So if we're anticipating lower prices for ES and it's not willing to
do this, that means we're extremely bullish. Go into your charts on the third week of May and the third week
of June.

If you like these charts, these are old friends of mine; they've been with me since 1995. This company is
headed by Steve Moore, and if you like seasonal tendencies or want to delve into that, his website is
www.mrci.com. I do not get anything for telling you that; I don't have an affiliation with him. He probably
doesn't even know who I am, though I've sent a ton of people to him. Steve, if you're listening, you're
welcome. I just think that he is the pinnacle of all seasonal tendency work. I don't care who it is, I don't care
who you like; his research, his work, is in my opinion, superior to everything and everyone else. So that's
why I use his resources for looking at seasonal tendencies. There is no business relationship between us.
But if you want seasonal tendency charts, his is the best; that's my opinion.

- VOLUME IMBALANCE & TRUE LIQUIDITY VOID
Here's a weekly chart of E-mini S&P, this is the September contract for 2023. There is the weekly volume
imbalance I mentioned multiple times, right between these two levels. That point is the exposed area where
there is no volume, making it a real gap. That, my friends, is a liquidity void. From here to here, that is not a
liquidity void. It's been unfortunately called that, and because I wanted to teach on BabyPips, I stuck with
the vernacular other people were using, to avoid causing more confusion, as I didn't want to go this far in
teaching at the time. But a real liquidity void is a void of all liquidity. That's what distinguishes me from
everybody else, because I will tell you, this is an inefficiency, it's a sell side imbalance, buy side inefficiency.
So it's inefficient in up or buy side offering. The market only went one side, ahead handily, and it's more
likely to offer movement on the upside. We see that over here on this candle. Each of these candles is a
weekly candlestick. We traded up into this gap, or real liquidity void, between this week's low and this
week's high. The majority of the entire weekly volume imbalance, we finally got to that one.

- CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT OF THE LIQUIDITY
VOID
Let's take a closer look right inside that small little gap. Before I go to the next slide, I want you to
understand that I'm going to zoom into this small segment of price action, and I'm going to show you the
relationship between this week's low here (this candle) and this week's high. We're going to measure that
with the Fibonacci, and we're going to get the midpoint of that, which is consequent encroachment,
because it's a gap. Here we are zoomed in; here's that candlestick we were looking at. The low for Monday,
April 4th, 2022, and then the 18th of April, 2022. This candlestick's high of its respective weekly candle, this
candlestick's low of its respective weekly candle. Drawing our Fib from that level to there, all we're doing is
using the Fib to get a perfect halfway point (50 percent). That means 4559 and a half. That is your
consequent encroachment of that small little gap that's right here, that little gap. So I'm finding half of this
between this red line and that red line. I want to know what the midpoint of that is, the exact midpoint.
That's what's being shown here: 4559 and a half.

Taking us over to the current present week, you can see how we traded right up into that consequent
encroachment here, a little above it, just by a little bit. But look how beautiful the reaction was from here,
back down, and we closed on the highs, not at the highs. We just fell short of going into the 20 retracement
for TGIF. All right, so let's go into a daily chart here. You can see the Fair Value Gap we mentioned at the
time: two down-closed candles, that's your bullish order block. Extend that to the right. At the start of the
week, we traded down into the order block and then ripped higher, ticking out buy side, digging into the low
of the weekly volume imbalance, the consequent encroachment of the entire shaded area (which is the
weekly volume imbalance), and in those two red levels up here, and then its respective consequent
encroachment or midpoint of that gap that's being shown within the weekly volume imbalance. Just a really
beautiful delivery for ES this week.

NASDAQ was actually better, but we'll look at that in a moment. But let's drop into a lower time frame here
for the hourly chart on ES. You see how we dropped down into your order block, rallied through a bullish
order block (this is down close here) and we have a Fair Value Gap that makes this a proper bullish order
block. Because we're in context of a buy program, we're looking for higher prices; everything should match
there. And we've seen a noon (lunchtime New York local time) return back into imbalance order block, sell
side taken flow, relative equal lows, and then it starts to trade higher. Another order block here trades
higher, an aggressive run through the short-term high here. So we're not looking at that as buy side, which
is resistance, right? No, we're looking at it going to it, being drawn to that area and then through it. And I
don't need it to trade back down to this high; that's classic support and resistance. I don't need that. I want
to see a trade inside of this inefficiency right here, right in here. That's institutional order flow entry drill, and
then it rallies again, trades up into that weekly volume imbalance. Look at the bodies respecting the weekly
volume imbalance. Yes, we have these little Mohawks; that's in-air price action, it's normal to see that, it's
perfectly normal and acceptable. Market rallies, trades up into the consequent encroachment of that actual
liquidity void in the upper portion of that shaded pink area, which is a weekly volume imbalance.

- SIBI, 2022 MODEL, SILVER BULLET & BREAKER TRADE
Here's a five minute chart. You can see again with the ES trading up into that consequent encroachment of
the actual liquidity void at the entire portion of that weekly volume imbalance. And right in here, you can
see a small little SIBI, which is a Fair Value Gap. It's also a balanced price range inefficiency. That means
the market will likely pull back up into that. It does, essentially, the consequent encroachment. We can see
those measurements here. Price trades to it here, and then we want to see a sharp displacement lower. Do
we get it? Yes. Did it take out a short term level? Yes. What is this? That's the 2022 model I taught you on
this YouTube channel. Fair Value Gap trades up into it here, boom. That's a short. What do you aim for?
The low under here. It delivers, and we have a small little Fair Value Gap there, offering the two o'clock
Silver Bullet. The market breaks lower, relative equal lows, we pull back up, retraces, and then aggressively
runs lower off of hitting a breaker. High, low, higher high. This candle is your breaker. Trades to it here, sells
off, going into the three o'clock hour. Small little Fair Value Gap there as well. So we have high, low, return
back in. This is Model 2022 again. Cell side hammers it in the final hour of trading on Friday.

- FAIR VALUE GAP AND TGIF SETUP CONTEXT
All right, here's NASDAQ. This is our weekly chart on NAS. You can see the weekly Fair Value Gap there.
We traded down into that two weeks ago, and this is this present week. So we opened, traded down,
aggressively moved higher, made a high, and then came off the highs. I want to zoom in on that. So we're
zoomed in on the weekly chart for NAS, and I'm taking the Fibonacci and measuring from the low of the
week to the high of the week, and I'm showing you the respective 20 and 30, which is TGIF.

- TGIF MODEL FOR WEEKLY RANGE RETRACEMENT
Whenever you have a nice big run higher or lower, those are the times you want to look for TGIF. They're
really likely to have these pullbacks towards the end of the week. And how far will it pull back? 20 to 30
percent of the weekly range entirely. So you can see that 15,698 and a half is 20 percent of the weekly
range for NASDAQ. So that level is going to be important going forward in this presentation. So 15,698 and
a half, that level's being noted here, and it's the weekly TGIF 20. It's a short little abbreviation for 'Thank
God It's Friday.' So it's a way for you to remember that that model is a day of week specific model, and it's
capitalizing on trading a reversal of the entire weekly range to a 20 to 30 percent level of the entire weekly
range. I have a teaching on it. If this is the first time you heard about it, just go on my YouTube channel, and
you'll find a TGIF setup.

- ADVANCED ICT BREAKERS FOR WEEKLY RANGE
PROJECTION
All right, so let's go into the hourly chart. We're going right to the brass tacks with this because it's already
a long video. I want you to take a look at the low here, the high, and the lower low. The market trades
through this high. We don't look at that as resistance. There's buy side above that, and we're looking for
higher prices beyond that. But what can we use this information for in terms of finding range? Well, if you
look at the teaching I gave you on Advanced ICT Breakers, those ideas that I shared in that video, I'm
applying it here. So when we see the stop run here that takes out the sell side below here, my eye goes
right to the low to the high prior to the lower low. It qualifies this as a bullish ICT breaker. So this is your
measuring range.

- FIBONACCI EXTENSIONS FOR WEEKLY HIGH PROJECTION
So you take your Fibonacci, apply it to the low to the high, extend it. Do multiple extensions. You can do
extension standard deviation negative one here, negative standard deviation two. Comes in at what price?
15,856 and a quarter. It's not the actual high, but look how close it gets you. That's projecting a weekly
Range High. Now there's people out there who say, 'Oh, well, you know, you can't do that, ICT. Nobody can
do that. The market's too random.' Nope, nobody can do that. Well, I'm teaching you tools and concepts
that, you know, I've been shown in my private group doing this for years and years of it. Now my students
can do it. You're learning how I'm able to do it. It's not imperative that you do this every single week. When
the technicals are there and you have a strong bias that is conducive for these ideas to pan out, that's
when you want to do it.

section_title: BREAKER LOGIC VS. RETAIL SUPPORT/RESISTANCE FALLACIES
content:
This breaker, we expected it to trade through it; it does. We come back down into it; the bodies are
respecting the breaker. It's not the fact that it goes up to this high and it repels price; it has absolutely
nothing to do with it. Has nothing to do with it, because if that's the case, why didn't it do it to him this high?
That's the fallacy of retail support and resistance ideas. If you're going to look at this high here and say,
'ICT is cherry-picking because it worked there,' well, use that same logic in saying that this is rebranded,
because it's not. Why don't you apply it to that high there, because it had a nice retracement off of it too. So
why is price not showing any willingness or respect of it over here? I think because it has nothing to do with
why price goes up or down.

- STOP RUNS, OFFSET DISTRIBUTION & MODEL 2022
The logic is this: short-term entries, those that are in pursuit of trying to go long, they were right
prematurely until they were stopped out. So this perfect trap here sets the stage for those individuals that
got stopped out trying to get long; they're too afraid to get back in, especially when there's one more
wipeout that takes out this low, which also took out that low. So we see a one, two, three drives pattern.
That is a classic retail pattern; yes, it does work if you have the context in the drawing of liquidity and where
you're trying to aim for. If that's correct, that pattern will work for you. If you see a level or a direction fail
after three attempts and it breaks down, and if you see a breaker or a Model 2022 inside of that, wow, what
a beautiful model. I probably said too much. The longs that have a sell stop below here, they get stopped
out; they're not going to go back in, they're scared. They're not going to get back in there. But now the
market will revert back to the individuals that are short here. Someone's short, so they're going to run on
them. So the sell stops are being accumulated by smart money going long. They're buying those sell stops
with the purpose of offsetting. So offset distribution is being used above this high. That's not support and
resistance; that's selling above a level for profit-taking and waiting for confirmation that it wants to go higher.
It does. The bodies are respecting that high, which is a breaker. It's not showing any willingness to show
the bodies respecting this high anywhere in here as support; it's not happening. So the market resumes
going higher. Pay no attention to this level yet, because it has to make a high of the week first. So when we
have this big capitulation, this is run up into a standard deviation of negative two on this range, which is
what I taught on that Advanced Breaker video that just literally just did it like a week or so ago on YouTube
channel, so watch that one and you'll see me teaching this very premise. That is beautiful delivery folks. I'm
sorry, I hate to break your hearts; I'm not trying to be mean, but this stuff isn't in your books. And if
someone goes out there and they want to run out and make an Amazon book and try to include this, you're
going to be coming up short-changed because there's a lot of things I'm leaving for this idea for my own
book. So sorry, not sorry. Then we have these relative equal lows, and the market draws down into that in
the latter portion of the day, but back down more specifically into the weekly TGIF 20.

- NEWS, GAPS, IOFED, SIBI & TGIF DRAW
So after we see this level hit, we want to see, does it really want to break down from that? And then when
we look at the lower time frames, we actually will see that actually occurring. So here's the 15-minute time
frame. Here's the consolidation in the beginning of the day, and we had a report come out that sends price
higher. Then we have this gap right there. So this return back into this SIBI, which is a cell side imbalance,
buy side inefficiency. Market trades into it here, we wick past it, that's completely permissible. Breaks down
again, we have another gap. So this is Institutional Order Flow Entry Drill (IOFED), where it just trades one
little bit above this candle here. This happens with all time frames, but the context is you have to know
where the market is likely to go. And because we have this gap here, and then we have this gap here in
close proximity, that's what makes this an Institutional Order Flow Entry Drill. Otherwise, if this wasn't here,
I would expect it to trade more into this inefficiency. I'll have more of that in my books, but that's enough
right here to send you light years ahead of everybody else. So the market does in fact trade below here,
completely runs over top of this buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency. It does it here, and we have
another gap there. We trade up into this and then rips right through sell side here and sell side there. So
we're not looking at that as buy because it was a turning point in the past for classic support and resistance
idea theories. We're looking for it to trade through it because there's liquidity below that. 20 percent of the
weekly range is here. So all of these things match in terms of a draw on liquidity. There's a reason to
anticipate it happening. The market's been going straight up all week, and then now we're at the end of the
week, so this is completely acceptable and reasonable to see in price delivery. And it's not to say that we've
called the top of the marketplace because, as I mentioned in July 9th, 2023's analysis, I'm teaching you not
to try to pick tops and bottoms on higher time frame charts because you can hurt yourself doing that. Just
wait for the market to clearly show you it's reversed, and when it does, just keep working that bias that's in
the market at the time, and try not to fancy dance it and try to pick a top or bottom because you're not
going to get it correct. You're not going to do it. So to avoid that, just simply try to stay on side and trade
with the expectation that the higher time frame weekly chart moving into a lower time frame daily chart,
that's enough for you to find opportunities and not need to be picking tops and bottoms.

Okay, here's a five minute chart, and I'll show you the Inversion Fair Value Gap. That's why the market went
up to that level here. It went beyond the previous 15-minute. Right here, see this gap here, this gap? We're
looking through that to the left. That's why we're not supply and demand folks, sorry Sam's Iden. This right
here is a buy side inefficiency. The market trades down through it now. And you might look at this and say,
'Oh, well, this is this is now it's filled in, we don't we don't worry about that.' That's why you're wrong,
because you're using flawed logic. My algorithm is going to refer back to that candle's high and that
candle's low; it will refer back to it again. That's not support and resistance; it's algorithmic. The market
trades back up into it. This is a small little in-air in price action; it's a Mohawk, it's a little nickname I gave it.
Small little, tiny little movement outside of it. There's no body outside of it; it's just the wick. That's
completely normal, completely acceptable. We don't worry about those things. Market shows its willingness
to respect the Inversion Fair Value Gap and then breaks lower. Institutional Order Flow Entry Drill. And then
we have this wick, big wick here, which doesn't do much in terms of damage below this low for sell side. So
once we move through it, I like this gap, but I wanted to refer back to this wick. So does this market respect
that wick's consequent encroachment? I don't know, does it? It's perfect, isn't it? Look at these candles
here; the bodies tell you the story, the wicks, they do the damage. And this is telling you a narrative: it
means the algorithm is in a cell program. It's going to seek sell side liquidity. It's going to run on sell stops.
It's going to run to inefficiencies. Well, there's a smaller inefficiency remaining here. It gets down to it there,
comes back up into a small gap there, and then does what? Dives for the sell side and gets below the
weekly TGIF 20 of the weekly range. I went short on here, trading inside of the idea that we were in this
Inversion Fair Value Gap there. I took a little bit of heat on it, but not much, and then aggressively ran for
the sell side. And there you go. So that's why I used to teach my son on Friday afternoon. Notice where we
closed; look at this, real close to the 20 percent. Again, you don't find that in your books.

- FORGING "NEW CLASSICS"
There are so many things that people will look at, whether it be me or other people, and because we're
looking at the same four things (open, high, low, and close), we're forced to use geometric shapes. We're
forced to use trend lines and things to articulate an idea with an audience member or for our own
journaling. And when we make these things available to other people, invariably, they're going to see, 'Oh,
well, you know, you're doing the classic this and the classic that.' I'm not doing anything that's classic; I'm
making the new classics. I'm making the new future reference books. Everything that you're learning here
live, you're in that Gann moment. You're in that Livermore moment. You're having someone teach you
something that no one's had their fingerprints on before. And I say these things not to be arrogant; I'm not
trying to be condescending to anybody, and I'm not saying to hear myself talk. But I want you to appreciate
the fact that what you're being exposed to, you're not entitled to it. You're not entitled to know these things.

section_title: THE VALUE AND CHALLENGE OF ICT MENTORSHIP
content:
So when I see other people claim they know it, claim they've been doing it before me, they have no
experience doing it, they have no track record with it. They can't even go out and do executions to prove
they don't even know how to do it right now, let alone say they did it before me. So I want you to think about
what you watched happen last week. How explosive the market moved, exactly how I outlined it last
Sunday before the market opened up. You had it in your hot little hands. What do you think your trading
would be like two years from now learning this? What do you think your trades would be like ten years from
now? What do you think your trading is going to be like three decades, like mine today? My understanding
of the marketplace is rooted on things that I've codified in 1996. So the things I'm talking about and
teaching, I author these things. No one taught me these things. No one mentored me. No one sat down and
said, 'Here, here's my mentorship, join this and I'm going to teach you that.' It wasn't in any of the books, it
wasn't in any of the things I bought. And it's not easy; I know it's not easy. You're learning it from the person
that created it. And it's a struggle sometimes for you to learn, and it's not because I'm not that good at
teaching, it's just this is a very dense topic. It's very, very, very dense. It requires a lot of study. It requires a
lot of case studies where you go back and look at old moves and you study it, and you listen to the boring
stuff I talk about, whether it be in a Twitter space or whether it be in these parts of the video where no one
really likes to listen to it. But you're not going to learn it without having these things, and that's what
mentorship is.

section_title: AVOIDING ABBREVIATED MENTORSHIP AND RECOGNIZING TRUE AUTHORITY
content:
If you want to sit down and learn something from somebody that knows something very well, and if they
really want to teach you, they're not going to rush through the topic. They want to make sure that they get
into all the nooks and crannies. They want to get down to the small little things that are going to creep in
and mess you up because you're going to make a big mountain out of something that's not that significant.
Now I know what those things are going to be, so that's the reason why I talk to you about these things.
Don't focus on that, focus on this. Do this, don't do that. And for the folks that want to speed through it and
try to do an abbreviated mentorship, you're the one that is going to fail. And I have a lot of failures because
they rushed through it, not because the content doesn't work or the concepts don't work or I can't teach it
properly. But there's going to be a huge learning curve for some of you, and others, you'll pick it up faster
than someone else might. It's just a matter of personal experience, and each one of you are going to have
a duration that is unique to you, and you have to submit to that. I'm a practical person; I know that not all of
you are going to be able to resonate with me. I'm not going to scratch the itch as a mentor for all of you,
and you're going to want to chase something else. I promise you folks, I promise you, I swear to you that
there is not one single person on YouTube or anywhere else that's going to teach you like I'm teaching you.
I promise you that. They're just familiar with what I've already shared thus far. So there's nothing wrong with
looking at other people's level of understanding, and that's what they're doing: they're showing you their
level of understanding. But they're not in a position of authority to be able to teach it because they don't
even know what they're teaching. They're just familiar with it. It's just like when you first got a driver's
license, and you handed, or your parents handed, the keys to you, and you get in and you start it up. Just
because you got your driver's license doesn't mean you understand exactly how that car is designed, what
causes the ignition, what causes the engine to stay running, what's all the connections that causes all of
these instruments to work like they do. You probably didn't do much thinking in terms of that; you just
couldn't wait to get behind the wheel. And that's what these mentors, and that's what you many times as a
would-be student, is like: you want to rush out there and just get your hands dirty with it, and sometimes it
ain't dirt, it's blood. You lose a few fingers, maybe more, because you're rushing in before you're ready, and
you're learning from people that don't know what they're doing. It's easy to talk about in hindsight. It's easy
to use what I'm talking about in real time analysis and then borrow my viewpoint in the marketplace and
then come out and say, 'You know, look at my look at my students that pass their evaluations,' when if I
wouldn't have said anything, you would have had no voice, and your students wouldn't be doing anything.

section_title: MARKET DELIVERY PROOF AND SEASONAL TENDENCY FAILURE AS CATALYST
content:
So, I'm going to leave that with you and remind you that these things aren't cherry-picked; these things are
not coincidence. They happen every single week, and sometimes there are certain parameters that are met
that allow for explosive runs. What took place this week? Think about it. What's occurring on the daily
chart? Fair Value Gap. Boom, third week of what? Third week of what? June. Just like that, seasonal
tendency calls for. Folks, those things are like road maps. And we knew in May, when the seasonal
tendency that I like to see (where if it sells off, if I can find something technical that aligns with the market
to see it go lower), if it's not providing that, that means we are really bullish. S&P went up over 400 handles.
Look at the NASDAQ: almost a thousand or more from this turning point right there. And then it rips again
in July. This move right here, this is a classic in the summer months. That part is a classic scenario. If
you're in a bull market, this is what you expect in the months of June and May. If we're in a bearish market,
a sell-off in May into June, they're very, very good. That's how you use these alternatives. The idea or a
premise behind a seasonal tendency that is failing to manifest itself, that is many times (not always, but
many times) it is conducive for an explosive price run in the opposite direction to what the seasonal
tendency would imply. I learned that from Larry Williams, and I learned that in 1995 from his four VHS
tapes I mentioned many times over the years as a resource that I learned from. And it's, I believe, titled
'The Future Millionaires Confidential Trading Course.' And he talked about seasonal tendencies in there. He
talked about how he was the first one to write a book about seasonal tendencies. And we talked about Jake
Bernstein, who I'm not a fan of; I never liked his work, even though Larry himself respected him, I didn't. I
didn't find anything of value from him. And I'm sure there were people that found usefulness from whatever
he put out, I just personally wasn't able to do that. But in terms of seasonal tendencies, the idea of when a
seasonal tendency is failing to manifest itself, that is many times not always but many times it is conducive
for an explosive price run in the opposite direction to what you would expect in the seasonal tendency. So
it's because we had the potential to sell off in May, didn't form, it did not form at that time. So that gives
more rocket fuel for the June low that forms, and that's classic in stock index futures.

section_title: UNIVERSAL APPLICATION OF ICT CONCEPTS AND DEBUNKING AMBIGUOUS S/R
content:
So as a swing trader, I'm saying all this stuff to you because I know some of you don't want to day trade.
You can't do it, you don't want to do it, it doesn't fit your personality, and I understand that. But what I'm
showing you here on this daily chart, how's that any different from what I'm teaching you on a one-minute
chart, four-minute chart, five-minute chart, 15-minute chart, hourly chart? It's nothing different except for
the time frame. Isn't this something that we would be looking to get long on anyway if we're bullish? Yes.
What is this here? High and that high? That's relative equal highs. That means what? There's buy side
above that. We're not looking at that as going back out through this here as resistance. We're looking for it
to trade through it. It doesn't sound like resistance to you. What kind of resistance would that be? If you
thought that this was a resistance level, these relative equal highs, you thought that same idea was the
same thing you would hope your roof on your home is like. Would you want a roof that performs like this?
No. So I attack, and I teach my students to attack buy side liquidity above a single or relative equal high. I
teach my students to attack a single low or relative equal lows where there's sell side below it, to it and
through it. That's what we teach here. We do not teach some garbage resistance and support idea that is
so ambiguous you have no idea. There's never been a book-I'm telling you right now, I have over 2,000
books, and there has never been an author, educator, teacher that has ever codified a single approach that
goes in and finds the right support and resistance. It's not there folks. That's a religion. That's what it is, it's
a religion. They say we're cult. The idea of support and resistance: you have to have more faith in that than
simply understanding that what I'm teaching here is the real market. Because it's subjective.

section_title: OBJECTIVITY OF ICT VS. SUBJECTIVITY OF S/R
content:
- PHANTOM TRENDLINE
No matter where you are in the world, if you're pulling up the NASDAQ daily chart, you're going to see this
gap. It's a Fair Value Gap in the form of a buy side imbalance, sell side inefficiency. That does not hide from
you. There are not other ones in this area that you might get tricked by. But the idea of picking a support
and resistance idea based on classic support and resistance theory, what high are you going to use? What
low are you going to use? What diagonal trend lines are you going to draw to and which one's going to be
correct? See, I've wasted lots of money doing that, though, and so have you. Other people that have traded
before I was even born wasted and lost lots of money doing that stuff too. But hey, it looks great in the
books. It looks great in hindsight. Look at this level here, this diagonal support, this diagonal resistance.
And I went in and I created a concept that attacks that idea, that entire premise. I attack it. That's my
Phantom trendline. It's a specific approach to going after retail thought processes about that very idea. I've
taken retail and turned it upside down, and I've made it useful to anyone that simply wants to look at it,
investigate the ideas, and see whether or not I'm telling you the truth. And if you go into charts, I promise
you, you're going to find that truth and that proof.

section_title: THE UNPRECEDENTED NATURE OF ICT'S MARKET TEACHING
content:
There are so many people, there are students of mine now, there are so many students of mine that used
to be detractors, doubters in the beginning. They want to go in and prove that what I was saying wasn't the
case. And now they're long-term, loyal students; they know what they have learned from me is the truth.
And you're seeing that, you just saw it again. How can you cherry-pick the entire weekly range? How can
you cherry-pick the entire weekly delivery of not just one market, but four? I gave you Forex, I gave you the
Dollar Index, and I gave you the ES. It delivered like gangbusters. And you should be excited about that, not
hopped up on goofballs, but you should be inspired by thinking, 'Well, you know, this is this is really
encouraging for me. I now have something to go on,' because everybody that watched that video on July
9th of 2023, that analysis video, the commentary that I gave in terms of, you know, what I felt the market
was going to do this week, it's on YouTube, it's available to everyone. You didn't have to pay for it. You don't
have to do any kind of subscriptions. You don't have to send me no money through PayPal. You ain't got to
do any kind of crypto payments, which I wouldn't take anyway. All you have to do is watch the videos, take
notes, and share your experience with me. That's it. It's free folks. And there's people out there that simply
want to keep you from learning how to do this. And the more closer we get to November, it's going to
increase because nothing else you're going to learn can compete with this. Nothing. I promise you, I
promise you, there is nothing like this.

section_title: THE CORE OF MARKET MOVEMENT AND MENTOR'S GRATITUDE
content:
Because this is the truth behind the marketplace. This is exactly why markets go up and down. It has
nothing to do with the depth of market. It has nothing to do with market profile. It has nothing to do with
supply and demand. It has nothing to do with Elliott Wave. It has nothing to do with harmonic patterns,
none of that stuff is what causes markets going up and down. That's not, there's no such thing as buying
and selling pressure. There's buying and selling going on, I'm not arguing that. But when the market goes,
it's going to a very specific level, and I'm teaching you what they are. I'm telling you when they're going to
do it. There has never been a phenomenon like what you're experiencing right now. And I'm honored, I'm
very honored, to be sitting at this helm with you as your mentor, at a point where I have 771,000 students
on this YouTube channel now. That to me is bonkers. It's it's crazy that there's that many people wanting to
sit down and listen to a guy from Middle River Maryland. So I'm definitely blessed, the Lord has definitely
made me the head, not the tail, and I'm greatly appreciative of all of you that want to sit down and spend
time. Until I talk to you next time.
